mnemonics.title: LINEAR SEARCH STEPS
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER: 'CHECK ONE BY ONE TILL YOU FIND OR END' - C-O-B-Y-O-F-Y-F-O-E - C=Compare, O=One, B=By, Y=One, O=One, F=Find, Y=Your, F=Target, O=Or, E=Exhaust all elements.

mnemonics.title: COMPLEXITY MEMORIZER
mnemonics.content: LINEAR = L-I-N-E-A-R = Lasts In Non-optimal Everything And is Repetitive - O(n) time complexity is its main characteristic.

acronyms.usage: Searching algorithm that checks each element sequentially
Another name for Linear Search emphasizing the sequential nature
Measure of algorithm execution time - O(n) for Linear Search
Measure of extra memory used - O(1) for iterative Linear Search

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a teacher checking attendance by calling each student's name one by one until finding the right student or reaching the end of the roll book.
Think of searching for your contact in a phone: Best case = first person you check is your contact; Worst case = it's the last one or not there at all.
Visualize index 0 to n-1 as a horizontal line of boxes. Linear Search moves left-to-right, checking each box until target is found.

analogies.analogy: Like looking for a specific book on an unsorted shelf - you have to check each book one by one from left to right.
Like reading a dictionary page by page to find a word instead of using the alphabetical index.
Like finding the first person with a specific name in a queue - you check each person until you find the first match, then stop.
